channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: MP Supports Baringo Boy After Humiliation Incident
item.description: A young boy from Baringo, identified as Dragon, was publicly humiliated at a public event after an MP removed his cap branded with Mogotio MP Reuben Kiborek's name. The incident went viral, sparking o...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial Begins with Debate on Presiding Officer
item.description: The impeachment trial of Sara Duterte, the daughter of former President Duterte, commenced on July 6, 2026, with the first session focusing on the selection of the presiding officer. The proceedings, ...

item.title: UBA Announces Tony Elumelu's Retirement and Emmanuel Nnorom's Appointment as New Chairman
item.description: United Bank for Africa (UBA) Plc has officially announced that its Group Chairman, Tony Elumelu, will retire from the bank’s Board of Directors on August 21, 2026. This decision follows the completion...

item.title: Kia Tasman 2026 Unveiled: Features and Market Positioning
item.description: The 2026 Kia Tasman, a double-cab bakkie powered by a 2.2l turbodiesel engine delivering 154kW of power and 440Nm of torque, has been reviewed by Ignition TV presenter Juliet McGuire. This vehicle is ...

item.title: China's push to develop dextrous robotic hands for practical applications
item.description: The article explores China's advancements in robotics, focusing on the development of dextrous robotic hands as a critical step towards creating functional humanoid robots. It highlights the complexit...

item.title: Trump Criticizes Kindergartners for Wearing Hijabs in Public School
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump posted a video of Muslim kindergartners wearing hijabs at a graduation ceremony, exposing their faces to criticize their religious attire. The footage, shared on Truth Soci...

item.title: Global Challenges in Economy, Politics, and Society
item.description: The article explores the interconnected challenges facing economies, political systems, and societal structures worldwide. It highlights how economic disparities, political instability, and social ine...

item.title: Edo Govt Reunites Deportee with Family After Rescue Operation
item.description: The Edo State Government has successfully reunited Peter Guobadia, a 53-year-old US deportee, with his family after rescuing him in Benin City. Guobadia was found wandering along Airport Road in a vis...

item.title: Sports Betting and Gambling's Global Expansion in the World Cup Era
item.description: The article explores how the FIFA World Cup has transformed into a massive global betting event, with punters wagering an estimated A$850 billion on the 104-game tournament. This shift reflects the br...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Case and Legal Defense Arguments
item.description: The impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte, which alleges violations of the Constitution, graft, and betrayal of public trust, has sparked legal debates over constitutional boundaries. H...

item.title: Southern Ontario group faces fines for illegal fishing activities in northern Ontario
item.description: A group from southern Ontario has been fined for engaging in illegal fishing activities in northern Ontario. The incident highlights ongoing challenges in enforcing fishing regulations in remote areas...

item.title: Government Reforms to ISAs Pose Challenges for First-Time Homebuyers
item.description: The article critiques recent government reforms to Individual Savings Accounts (ISAs), particularly the replacement of the Lifetime ISA (Lisa) with a First-Time Buyer (FTB) ISA. The author, a young fi...

item.title: Man in Kileleshwa laundromat steals phone via CCTV after leaving it unattended
item.description: CCTV footage captured a man in Kileleshwa leaving a smartphone unattended at a laundromat during lunch hours. The man, who appeared to be posing as a hawker, placed small bottles on the counter and wa...

item.title: Trevor Gumbi pays tribute to Johannesburg through Ponte Tower on ‘The Masked Singer SA’
item.description: Comedian Trevor Gumbi's participation in The Masked Singer SA Season 3 has sparked conversations about Johannesburg's cultural identity. Gumbi, who was unmasked as the first celebrity to exit the comp...

item.title: Nigeria and South Africa Tackle Rising Anti-Migrant Violence
item.description: Nigeria has confirmed the deaths of two citizens in South Africa, raising concerns about foreign national safety amid escalating anti-migrant sentiment. The victims, Emeka Charles Iroegbu and Musa Yun...

item.title: Government accuses JAAC of anti-Pakistan agenda linked to Indian funding in AJK
item.description: Azad Jammu and Kashmir Information Department Secretary Muhammad Rashid Hanif claimed state institutions have credible evidence of Indian funding supporting the banned Joint Awami Action Committee (JA...

item.title: Police increase patrols after repeated motorbike vandalism at Canberra region sportsgrounds
item.description: Authorities in the Canberra region have stepped up police patrols following a series of vandalism incidents involving motorbikes and other small vehicles damaging local sportsgrounds. The latest incid...

item.title: Gauteng achieves 131% HIV prevention target with Lenacapavir rollout
item.description: The Gauteng health department has surpassed its first-month target for distributing Lenacapavir, a long-acting HIV prevention medication, with 6,130 eligible individuals initiated between June 8 and t...

item.title: NAB Seizes Bahria Icon Tower in Malik Riaz Anti-Money Laundering Case
item.description: The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) has taken physical possession of Karachi’s Bahria Icon Tower, valued at around Rs100 billion, as part of an ongoing investigation into property tycoon Malik Ri...

item.title: Educational Events and Reunions in Dindigul Colleges
item.description: The article highlights various educational initiatives and events across colleges in Dindigul and surrounding areas. Key activities include a national-level workshop organized by ANJAC and the Nationa...
